name: Fresh and Salt Cod Cakes/Jalapeno Tartar/3
recipeInstructions:
Notes: On Cod, soak in water overnight, changing the water several times and rinsing and draining. To determine if cod has soaked enough, taste the soaking water. The cod is soaked if the mixture is slightly salty and the fish has plumped up to nearly double its original size. Soaking cod too long results in a fish with no flavor.
1. In a large saucepan, mix together the soaked and drained salt cod, water, and milk.
2. Place the bay leaf, thyme, and parsley in a 4-inch square piece of cheesecloth and tie it securely with string. Put the spice bag in the cod, water, and milk mixture.
3. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and cook for 15 minutes.
4. Add the fresh cod and cook for an additional 5 minutes. Drain and remove the fish to a large bowl to cool; discard the flavoring ingredients.
5. Flake the fish with a fork and set aside. In a large skillet, melt butter until sizzling, add celery and chopped onion, and cook until the onions are translucent (about 5 minutes).
6. Add the cooked vegetables to the flaked fish, then add egg, green onions, chives, parsley, cayenne pepper, Tabasco sauce, dried mustard, Worcestershire sauce, mayonnaise, lemon juice, bread crumbs, salt, and pepper to taste.
7. Form the mixture into 6 to 8 patties (3-4 inches each). Place on a baking sheet and cover; refrigerate for 1 hour.
8. If the mixture is too wet, add more breadcrumbs as needed.
9. Whisk together egg yolks and milk in a small bowl. In a shallow pie plate, combine cornmeal and flour.
10. Dip cod cakes into flour mixture, then in egg and milk mixture, and finally dredge them in remaining bread crumbs. Repeat with the remaining cod cakes.
11. In a large skillet, melt butter and oil together. Fry half of the cod cakes about 3-4 minutes per side until fairly brown.
12. Drain fishcakes on paper towels. Repeat with the remaining cakes.
13. Serve warm with Jalapeno Tartar Sauce and fresh lemon or lime wedges.
